observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management facilitate the immediate and full
implementation of the one-time cleansing of Property, Plant, and Equipment (PPE)
accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
We further recommended that Management direct the following:
a. The OIC-Property Officer to:
i. ensure the complete and timely maintenance of the Report on the
Physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment (RPCPPE) and
corresponding Property/Equipment Cards (PECs) to support effective
reconciliation with accounting records; and
ii. submit the RPCPPE in accordance with the deadline prescribed under
the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for
Local Government Units (LGUs).
b. The Accounting Office to:
i. individually verify and validate all PPE items to confirm that only those
meeting the ₱50,000.00 capitalization threshold are recognized as PPE,
and avoid aggregating the costs of semi-expendable items not acquired
in bulk for purposes of capitalization;
ii. review and update the depreciation/lapsing schedule to ensure that all
depreciable assets are properly subjected to systematic depreciation, in
accordance with applicable accounting standards; and
iii. prepare and record the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the
deficiencies noted in the classification, recognition, and depreciation of
PPE.

Management was amenable to the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management:

a. direct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to:
i. maintain a sequential log of Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) using
the format prescribed in COA Circular No. 2022-004;
ii. use the ICS to accurately prepare the Registry of Semi-Expendable
Property Issued (RegSPI);
iii. prepare and submit the weekly Report of Semi-Expendable
Property Issued (RSPI) to the Accounting Office; and
iv. prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Semi-
Expendable Property (RPCSP) in a timely manner, ensuring
completeness and accuracy of reported details.
b. require the Accounting Office to prepare Journal Entry Vouchers to
recognize the issuance of semi-expendable properties as expense, based on
the weekly RSPI submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office; and
c. instruct both the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and Accounting Office to
periodically reconcile their records to promptly identify and correct
discrepancies.

Management was amenable to the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management enjoin the OIC-Property/Supply Officer, Municipal Accountant, and other concerned department heads, to comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the Local Roads Asset Management System outlined in COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended that Management direct:

a)	The Municipal Accountant and OIC – Property/Supply Officer to ensure that SLCs and SCs are maintained, the physical count of inventories is done semestrally and the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories is prepared accordingly, as prescribed in Volume I of MNGAS for LGUs; and

b)	The OIC – Property/Supply Officer to prepare and submit weekly consolidated Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) together with the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to the Municipal Accountant to timely record the appropriate expense accounts for the issuance of inventories, as enjoined under Section 121, Volume I of MNGAS for LGUs.

We likewise recommended that Management designate permanent personnel who will be in charge of monitoring the receipt and issuance/utilization of supplies and materials.
observations.managementAction: Inventory Committee was created but no designated permanent personnel to monitor the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials.
observations.reason: Lack of Personnel

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to: 

a)	Review the composition of the Accounts Payable to determine the valid claims and ensure that all recorded payables are duly supported with the required documents; and

b)	Henceforth, conduct regular analysis, review, and monitoring of all booked obligations to ensure that all accounts payables are immediately settled, and only valid claims with supporting documents remain in the books.
observations.managementAction: Recently recorded payables are duly supported with the required documents while long outstanding balances have not yet been resolved due to difficulties in verifying claims due to lack of creditor’s information.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a)	Through the Sangguniang Bayan, facilitate the grant of authority to apply for the reactivation of the dormant bank account; and

b)	Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to return the remaining undisbursed balance of the BGCM Fund as of December 31, 2021, amounting to ₱846,520.98 to the National Treasury as enjoined under Item 5.0 DBM Circular Letter No. 2021-13 dated December 31, 2021.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Health Office, Property/Supply Office, and Accounting Office to coordinate with each other to align their inventory control procedures with existing rules and regulations; and facilitate the proper recording of the COVID-19 vaccines and ancillary supplies in the books of accounts as well as in other inventory records

We recommended that Management facilitate the submission of the certifications required under Paragraph B (5) of COA Resolution No. 2021-044 dated December 28, 2021, to support the payment of claims for travel expenses by the JO/COS personnel.

We recommended that Management:

a)	Ensure that MOA shall be secured first as required under COA Circular No. 94-013, before undertaking projects involving inter-agency fund transfers;

b)	Instruct the personnel concerned to conduct periodic analysis, review, and monitoring of all fund transfers to ensure compliance with all requirements stipulated in the MOA as well as in other relevant laws and regulations; and

c)	Require the Municipal Accountant to individually verify and validate the SL account balances relating to various fund transfers to establish rights and obligations therefrom and effect the necessary adjusting journal entries, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Similar deficiencies were observed for CY 2023 transactions

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a)	Stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to officers and employees with outstanding cash advances;

b)	Institute appropriate legal actions/sanctions for non-settlement and non-liquidation of cash advances following the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-004 and other existing laws and regulations, if warranted; and

c)	Ensure that the granting, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances is in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction:
Some cash advances are vital for government operations

Demand Letters were already sent to concerned officers and employees but some have not yet responded to settle their cash advances.
Granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances are guided by COA Circular No. 97-002 but some cash advances are vital for government operations, so even though previous CA of the Disbursing Officer are not yet liquidated, cash advances were granted to support different programs, projects, and activities of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to faithfully observe its duties and responsibilities relative to the procurement activities; and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursement vouchers are supported with complete documentation following COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that Management ensure that procurement of gasoline, oil and lubricants strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 122, Volume I of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for the proper sequence of control of inventory particularly in the procurement of said goods, as well as the principle of competitiveness enshrined under Section 3 of R.A. 9184 by extending equal opportunity to enable private contracting parties who are eligible and qualified to participate in a public bidding.
observations.managementAction: Some end users submitted their PRs of fuel expenses after its consumption. BAC conducted orientation to all end users regarding updates and procurement process.
observations.reason: Not Implemented | Some end users submitted their PRs of fuel expenses after its consumption. BAC conducted orientation to all end users regarding updates and procurement process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the concerned personnel to:

a)	Regularly monitor the utilization of the funds so that these are spent for their intended use/activity, and ensure accurate reporting of actual cost in the GAD Accomplishment Report; 


b)	Ensure that any changes in the GPB are properly communicated to the DILG and proper procedures are followed before effecting such changes; and 

c)	Prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report, observing the procedures indicated in the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 (as amended).

We likewise recommended that Management establish and maintain a gender-responsive monitoring and evaluation system by creating a Monitoring and Evaluation Team and/or instructing the GAD Focal Person to use available monitoring and evaluation tools indicated in the same JMC.
observations.reason: Lack of proper monitoring of implementation of GAD PPAs

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the GFPS Executive Committee, with proper coordination of all departments involved, to exert best effort in order to ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the GAD programs, projects and activities and the judicious utilization of the GAD Budget.
observations.reason: Similar deficiencies were observed for CY 2023

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management adopt strategies to implement the development projects within the targeted timeframe such as, but not limited to, the following:

a)	Develop a concrete plan that will articulate the development projects, timeline, expected deliverables for each phase, key milestones, etc.; 

b)	Evaluate thoroughly the proposed priority development projects to be funded under the 20 percent DF as to their readiness for implementation or procurement to avoid delays or non-implementation thereof; and

c)	Direct the LGU’s Engineer and Planning and Development Coordinator to monitor periodically the implementation of development projects funded under the 20 percent DF.

We likewise recommended that Management refrain from disbursing the 20% Municipal Development Funds for programs, projects, and activities not included in the Municipal Development Plan.
observations.reason: Similar deficiencies were observed for CY 2023

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to reconcile the subsidiary schedules of Construction in Progress (CIP) accounts with the list/report of on-going construction projects as of year-end, and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries for all discrepancies such that beginning balances which can no longer be ascertained shall be treated as prior period adjustment closed to government equity account, and completed projects shall be reclassified to their appropriate PPE accounts with corresponding adjustment on depreciation, so that only the costs of all on-going projects with supporting details/schedules will remain recorded in the CIP accounts.
observations.managementAction: Necessary adjusting journal entries were made for those projects identified and ongoing reconciliation for the remaining balances.
observations.reason: The balance of CIP accounts includes beginning balances which could not be traced.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management, through their responsible Municipal personnel:

Require every contractor of the infrastructure project to prepare an “As Built” Plan, a new set of drawings accurately depicting each and every part of the structures/facilities as actually built of the completed project and submit the same observing timeliness of submission;

Prepare and approve the Statement of Work Accomplished based on contractor-prepared As-Built Plan; and

Facilitate the submission of the Approved Original Plan with color coded indication of its revision and the revised PERT/CPM Network Diagram which shall also be color coded.
observations.reason: Similar deficiencies were observed for CY 2023 procurement transactions

observations.managementAction: Not Implemented | Similar practice was observed for CY 2023 transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to compute and require the payment of liquidated damages based on the provision of Paragraph 8.1, Annex E of the RIRR of R.A. No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Liquidated damages were computed, but no payment has been made

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers together with its complete supporting documents in order that a proper review of transactions can be achieved.
observations.managementAction: Some DVs out of ₱27.187 million are not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Bids and Awards Committee and Municipal Accountant to observe due diligence in the discharge of their functions with regard to the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects, as well as the processing of claims.
observations.managementAction: Lack of BAC permanent personnel to assist BAC Secretariat and Procurement Unit in preparation examination and submission of disbursement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to submit promptly all contracts with their supporting documents to the Audit Team for review within five days from perfection thereof in compliance with existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: Lack of BAC permanent personnel

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Strictly adhere to the provisions of R.A. 9003 and implement the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan they had prepared and allot a budget for the program; 

As a first step, put up receptacles that are well categorized and segregated and immediately take a comprehensive program to address garbage disposal that would ultimately eliminate the existing practice; and

Take appropriate actions that would lead to the closure of the open dump and switch to sanitary landfill.
observations.managementAction:
The revised plan was submitted to the Environmental Management Bureau on May 13, 2024, for evaluation and approval.



The LGU has effected closure of its dumpsite and is currently operating a Temporary Residual Containment Area. Residual wastes are collected by Iwa Besu Corporation and brought to its disposal site in Daraga, Albay.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct:

a)	The Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments in the books to record the reconciling items of the cash in bank account; and

b)	The Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the cashbooks for cash in banks were daily updated and balanced by the Accountable Officers, and regularly tallied with the account balances in the General Ledger of the Accounting Office.

We recommended that Management exhaust all possible remedies to enforce collection of delinquent taxes especially those prescribed under Sections 256 of RA 7160 or the remedies by administrative action thru levy on real property or by judicial action to lessen the accumulation of tax delinquencies and increase the collection efficiency of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:

a)	Instruct the LDRRM Council to review and update annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) to include the programs, projects, and activities to be funded from the unexpended balance of LDRRMF pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM JMC No. 2013-1; and

b)	Ensure that the LDRRMF, including the unexpended balances of previous years, is utilized only for programs, projects, and activities incorporated in the LDRRMFIP to avoid irregularity in the disbursements of the same.
observations.reason: Implemented |  | For CY 2023, a Supplemental LDRRMFIP was prepared, which includes programs to be funded from the unexpended balance of LDRRMF

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in his capacity as Chairman of the Local School Board, instruct the personnel concerned to strictly adhere to the schedule of activities in the preparation and approval of the SEF Budget observing timeliness in the preparation and submission of the documents and records necessary in planning the PPAs to be charged against the Special Education Fund.
observations.reason: Similar deficiencies were observed for CY 2023

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to assess whether the items with negative balances in the receivable and payable accounts still has valid claims from various creditors/officers/employees and the LGU, respectively, and effect the necessary adjustments/corrections, if warranted, in order to reflect the appropriate balances of the said accounts.
observations.managementAction: Correcting entries were made in the books to eliminate the negative balances of receivable and payable accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to address the problems which caused the erroneous and/or unreconciled records in the Due to NGAs account as well as the negative balances and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Necessary adjusting journal entries were made for those erroneous entries identified. But reconciliation is still on-going for the remaining balances

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Property/Treasurer’s Office and Accounting Office to:
Comply with the required preliminary activities prior to inventory taking as part of the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts, and henceforth, ensure full implementation of the cleansing process as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and
Exert efforts to reconcile their records on a regular basis so that discrepancies thereon may be immediately corrected.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in his capacity as the Chairman of the LDRRMC, facilitate the formulation of the three to five years Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan and ensure proper implementation of the programs, projects or activities embodied in the LDRRMFIP through strict adherence to the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 10121.

We recommended that Management revert the unexpended balance of LDRRMF from CYs 2015 and prior years totaling ₱6,637,612.52 back to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund for subsequent re-appropriation by the local sanggunian for other social services.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Development Council to judiciously plan the programs/projects/activities (PPAs) to be funded out of the 20 per cent Development Fund and allocate the Fund to a reasonable number of PPAs or only those of utmost priority which can be probably undertaken within the year, taking into account the estimated amount of time spent for the procurement and all other factors affecting the implementation process; and
Advise the Municipal Engineer to intensify control and supervision over the infrastructure projects, conduct frequent inspection of all municipal works and regularly monitor the status of all projects in order to immediately address causes of failure or delay in the implementation thereof.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure that funds are used exclusively for the specific purpose for which the same were appropriated, in compliance with Section 336 of R.A. No. 7160; and
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from obligating the 20 per cent DF for programs, projects and activities which are not prescribed under DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 such as the purchase of a motor vehicle intended for administrative purposes.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Draw the journal voucher to record the valid reconciling items in order to reflect the correct balance of the Cash in Bank account in the books;
Review the transactions recorded under the accounts with negative balances and effect the necessary adjustments/corrections; and
Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the cancellation of stale checks and restore the amounts to the Cash in Bank account.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to reconcile the subsidiary schedules of Construction in Progress (CIP) accounts with the list/report of on-going construction projects as of year-end, and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries for all discrepancies such that beginning balances which can no longer be ascertained shall be treated as prior period adjustment closed to government equity account, and completed projects shall be reclassified to their appropriate PPE accounts with corresponding adjustment on depreciation, so that only the costs of all on-going projects with supporting details/schedules will remain recorded in the CIP accounts.

We recommended that Management instruct the:
Municipal Accountant to address the problems which caused the erroneous and/or unreconciled records in the Due to NGAs account as well as the negative balances and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, if warranted; and
Municipal Treasurer to return the amount of ₱100,000.00 allotted as Calamity Assistance during Typhoon Tisoy to the National Government.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to assess whether the items with negative balances in the receivable and payable accounts still has valid claims from various creditors/officers/employees and the LGU, respectively, and effect the necessary adjustments/corrections, if warranted, in order to reflect the appropriate balances of the said accounts.

We recommended that Management refrain from resorting to Negotiated Procurement-Emergency Cases for purchase of goods wherein no emergency is sought to be addressed and strictly adhere to the provisions of the 2016 RIRR of R.A. No. 9184 prescribing the guidelines regarding resort to said alternative mode of procurement.

We recommended that Management ensure that procurement of gasoline, oil and lubricants strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 122, Volume I of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for the proper sequence of control of inventory particularly in the procurement of said goods, as well as the principle of competitiveness enshrined under Section 3 of R.A. 9184 by extending equal opportunity to enable private contracting parties who are eligible and qualified to participate in a public bidding.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to compute and require the payment of liquidated damages based on the provision of Paragraph 8.1, Annex E of the RIRR of R.A. No. 9184; and
Review all existing infrastructure contracts entered into by the Municipality to ensure that the work will be accomplished and completed in accordance with the conditions stipulated in the contract agreement.

We recommended that Management require the GFPS Executive Committee, with proper coordination of all departments involved, to exert best effort in order to ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the GAD programs, projects and activities and the judicious utilization of the GAD Budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in his capacity as the Co-Chairperson of the Local School Board, facilitate the inclusion of programs, projects or activities related to Early Childhood Care and Development (ECCD) Programs in the Annual Budget of the Special Education Fund for the ensuing year, observing strict compliance with the provisions of R.A. 10410 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No.1, series of 2017.

We recommended that Management direct the Bids and Awards Committee and Municipal Accountant to observe due diligence in the discharge of their functions with regard to the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects, as well as the processing of claims.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to review the composition of the Accounts Payable to determine the valid claims and ensure that all recorded payables are duly supported with the required documents.
observations.reason: Lack of creditor’s information especially those long outstanding balances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all PPE and prepare the RPCPPE properly including maintenance of property cards and include all properties in the RPCPPE and Property/Equipment Cards ensuring that the reports contain adequate details/ description, property number, remarks on the condition and whereabouts of PPE and other information necessary to facilitate identification and reconciliation of records;

Direct the Accounting Office to maintain an updated PPE Ledger Cards in order to completely account for each item composing the PPE, investigate on the discrepancies noted and effect the necessary adjustments in the books; and

Designate on a full-time basis at least one personnel each from the Property and Accounting Offices to reconcile the RPCPPE, Property Cards, Property Ledger Cards, and accounting records, taking into consideration the noted deficiencies in audit. A cut-off date as of December 31, 2020 may be started to facilitate current reconciliation, then move back to earlier years.
observations.reason:
Absence/lack of documents/records for tracing of physical items




Ongoing tracing of records of those PPE acquired long ago. 


There is an ongoing reorganization of inventory committee.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Draw the journal voucher to record the valid reconciling items in order to adjust the balance of the cash in bank account; and

Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the cancellation of stale checks and restore the amounts to the Cash in Bank – LCCA.

We likewise recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to payees one month before the check become stale as required under Section 59 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Sent notices thru text messages
observations.reason: Difficulties in obtaining documents that will constitute sufficient basis in preparing journal entries since most of the balances were long outstanding in the Bank Reconciliation Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management issue final demand letters to the concerned officials or employee for the liquidation/settlement of their long outstanding cash advances and institute legal action against accountable officers which remained with unliquidated cash advances after the lapse of the period provided in the final demands.
observations.reason:
80% of these CA has been liquidated.

Other Accountable Officers with unliquidated cash advances were no longer in the service in the LGU or were already deceased.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: 

Provide a stock room for proper storage of items of inventories; 

Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the semi-annual physical inventory of all supplies and materials inventory of the LGU and to prepare the report thereon, copy furnished the Auditor within the prescribed period; and 

Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain Subsidiary Ledger Cards and Subsidiary Ledgers for the Inventory accounts.
observations.reason:
Only temporary stock room provided.

There is an ongoing reorganization of inventory committee that can function well.

Lack of personnel.

observations.reason: The reason for most amount as to why it became negative were discovered and for adjustments/corrections after verifying the details.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management, through their responsible Municipal personnel:

Require every contractor of the infrastructure project to prepare an “As Built” Plan, a new set of drawings accurately depicting each and every part of the structures/facilities as actually built of the completed project and submit the same observing timeliness of submission;

Prepare and approve the Statement of Work Accomplished based on contractor-prepared As-Built Plan; and

Facilitate the submission of the Approved Original Plan with color coded indication of its revision and the revised PERT/CPM Network Diagram which shall also be color coded.

Require the Municipal Development Council to judiciously plan the programs/ projects/activities to be funded out of the 20% EDF in accordance with the mandate of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1; and

Ensure that all DF projects proposed in the Annual Investment Plan of the LGU are fully implemented within the budget year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from charging against the 20% Development Fund PPAs which are either: a) mere transfers of funds not considered as utilization thereof; or b) DRRM-related which are not included in the allowable expenditures under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 (as amended).

Draw the journal voucher to record the valid reconciling items in order to adjust the balance of the cash in bank account; and
Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the cancellation of stale checks and restore the amounts to the Cash in Bank – LCCA.
We likewise recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to payees one month before the check become stale as required under Section 59 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs.

We recommended and Management agreed to issue final demand letters to the concerned officials or employee for the liquidation/settlement of their long outstanding cash advances and institute legal action against accountable officers which remained with unliquidated cash advances after the lapse of the period provided in the final demands.

We recommended that Management:
Provide a stock room for proper storage of items of inventories;
Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the semi-annual physical inventory of all supplies and materials inventory of the LGU and to prepare the report thereon, copy furnished the Auditor within the prescribed period; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain Subsidiary Ledger Cards and Subsidiary Ledgers for the Inventory accounts.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to review the items of the aforementioned Inter-Agency and Other Payables accounts with negative balances and effect the necessary adjustments/corrections in order to reflect the appropriate balances of the said liability accounts.

We recommended that Management, through their responsible Municipal personnel:
Require every contractor of the infrastructure project to prepare an “As Built” Plan, a new set of drawings accurately depicting each and every part of the structures/facilities as actually built of the completed project and submit the same observing timeliness of submission;
Prepare and approve the Statement of Work Accomplished based on contractor-prepared As-Built Plan; and
Facilitate the submission of copy of Warranty Security for the completed infrastructure project, the Approved Original Plan with color coded indication of its revision and the revised PERT/CPM Network Diagram which shall also be color coded.
We recommended that Management immediately refund the amount of ₱138,978.00 which are considered expenditures not allowed to be charged against the BGCM Fund to avoid disallowance in audit.
We recommended that Management refrain from utilization of the BGCM Fund for disbursements not allowed under the DBM LBC No. 125 dated April 7, 2020.

We recommended that Management require the GFPS Executive Committee to exert best effort in order to ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the GAD programs, projects and activities and the judicious utilization of the GAD Budget.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:


     a) Stop the granting of  additional  cash  advances  to  officers  and
        employees unless the previous ones are fully settled;


     b) Institute appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those  erring
        accountable officers/employees for  continued  non-compliance  with
        existing laws, rules and regulations on the  granting,  liquidation
        and utilization of cash advances, if warranted; and

     c) Ensure that the  granting,  utilization  and  liquidation  of  cash
        advances is in accordance with the provisions of COA  Circular  No.
        97-002.

We recommended that Management:


   a. create a  committee  composed  of  one  regular  personnel  each  from
      property and accounting who shall reconcile on a full time basis,  the
      submitted Report of Physical Count of Property,  Plant  and  Equipment
      (RPCPPE) with the accounting records  to  identify  any  discrepancies
      that may require adjustments; and

   b. designate a regular employee each from property and accounting offices
      who shall update and prepare the respective property  records  of  all
      items of PPE on a full time basis.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:


     a. Send confirmation letters to creditors to prove  the  existence  of
        these liability accounts; and


     b. Ensure that claims are substantiated with complete documentation.

We recommended that Management:


     a) cause the immediate settlement of all existing fund  transfers  for
        liquidation; and


     b) in future transactions, require the liquidation of  fund  transfers
        once projects are fully implemented and/or  refund  the  unutilized
        amount from  completed  projects  for  subsequent  and  appropriate
        recording in the books of accounts.

We recommended that Management direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to
submit immediately the disbursement  vouchers  together  with  its  complete
supporting documents in order that a proper review of  transactions  can  be
achieved.

We recommended that Management:

     a) Stop the granting of  additional  cash  advances  to  officers  and
        employees unless the previous ones are fully settled;


     b) Institute appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those  erring
        accountable officers/employees for  continued  non-compliance  with
        existing laws, rules and regulations on the  granting,  liquidation
        and utilization of cash advances, if warranted; and

     c) Ensure that the  granting,  utilization  and  liquidation  of  cash
        advances is in accordance with the provisions of COA  Circular  No.
        97-002.

We recommended that Management require the: (a) submission of  updated
and complete RPCPPE  and  other  records;  and  (b)  reconciliation  of  the
submitted  Report  of  Physical  Count  of  Property,  Plant  and  Equipment
(RPCPPE) with the accounting records to identify any discrepancies that  may

     b. Ensure that claims are substantiated with complete documentation.

documents to the Audit Team for review  within  five  days  from  perfection

vehicles.

observations.recommendation: To promptly implement all the programs/projects/activities as embodied in the approved Annual Development Plan in order to optimize the delivery of basic services to the people, we recommended that Management: (a) require the Municipal Development Council to judiciously plan the programs/projects/activities to be funded out of the 20% EDF in accordance with the mandate of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011; and (b) prosecute the projects through straight contract with terms and conditions indicating definite date of completion.
observations.managementAction: No action was made by Management to implement these recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) semesterly collate any available records/documents that would be the basis of the Accounting Office in reverting back to Office Supplies Inventory and Drugs and Medicines Inventory account the amount of P690,962.47 and P3,100,592.60 respectively; (b) semestrally conduct an inventory of supplies or property as required under Section 124 of Manual on NGAS for LGU; (c) maintain stock cards and supplied ledger card for each type of supplies; (d) record and issue supplies and materials following the perpetual inventory method as prescribed under Sections 114, 116 and 121 of the NGAS for LGUs; and (e) require the Municipal Accountant to revert back to Office Supplies Inventory and Drugs and Medicines Inventory account the records/documents that would be the basis of the Accounting Office in dropping from the books of the Municipality the non-existing Office Supplies Inventory and Drugs and Medicines Inventory.
observations.reason: No action was made by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit legal basis for the payment of the additional PEI to forestall the issuance of a Notice of Suspension.
observations.managementAction: No legal basis was submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Accountant to verify the status and validity of the accounts payable account balance which has been outstanding for two years or more and if no actual claim has been filed by the creditors, revert the account to the unappropriated surplus as required under Section 98 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: No action was made by Management to implement this recommendation.
observations.reason: No justification was given by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the officers of the two grantee associations to submit the Approved Summary of Budgetary Requirements detailing the items of expenditure/disbursements to be incurred in the program/project and liquidation reports on the utilization of the financial assistance granted.
observations.managementAction: The Management implemented the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (1) designate a social worker who will do the evaluating process in determining the eligibility of the prospective beneficiaries; (2) follow strictly the guidelines in the implementation of AICS program as provided under DSWD AO No. 5; (3) ensure that claims for financial assistance are properly supported with valid and complete documents; and (4) require the submission of the noted lacking documents and justification on the excessive grants to forestall the issuance of Notices of Suspension.
observations.managementAction: The Management implemented the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the above-noted supporting documents were attached to the payroll before processing and/or payment of labor payrolls are effected.
observations.managementAction: The Management continued the practice of processing the labor payrolls despite the absence of the documents needed to support the claim.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the BAC and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that complete supporting documents are attached before processing the disbursement vouchers in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The Management did not submit the complete supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Accountant not to process any claim for honoraria and RATA without the required supporting documents as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The Management continued the practice of processing the claim for honoraria despite the absence of documents needed to support the claim.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) strictly adhere to the provisions of R.A. 9003 and implement the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan they had prepared and allot a budget for the program; (b) as a first step, put up receptacles that are well categorized and segregated and immediately take a comprehensive program to address garbage disposal that would ultimately eliminate the existing practice; and (c) take appropriate actions that would lead to the closure of the open dump and switch to sanitary landfill.
observations.managementAction: The Management made no action to implement this recommendation.
observations.reason: No justification was given by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to provide the Accounting Section with the certified list of taxpayers with the name and amount due and collectible for the year as basis in the setting up of the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables at the beginning of the year by the Accountant.
observations.managementAction: The Management implemented the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the submission of the accounts at the earliest possible time in order that a proper review of transactions can be achieved.
observations.managementAction: The Management made no action to implement this recommendation.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) conduct a periodic physical inventory semestrally as required under Section 124 of Manual on NGAS for LGU; (b) maintain stock cards and supplies ledger cards for each type of supplies; (c) record and issue supplies and materials following the perpetual inventory method as prescribed under Sections 114, 116 and 121 of the NGAS for LGUs; and (d) require the Municipal Accountant to revert back to Office Supplies Inventory and Drugs and Medicines Inventory account the amount of $690,962.47 and $3,100,592.60 respectively or collate any available records/documents that would be the basis of the Accounting Office in dropping from the books of the Municipality the non-existing Office Supplies Inventory and Drugs and Medicines Inventory.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 page 27
observations.reason: Lack of personnel

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit legal basis for the payment of the additional PEI to forestall the issuance of a Notice of Suspension.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 page 30
observations.reason: No legal basis was submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Accountant to verify the status and validity of the accounts payable account balance which has been outstanding for two years or more and if no actual claim have been filed by the creditors, revert the account to the unappropriated surplus as required under Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

observations.reason: Accounts Payable aging over 2 years increase by P4,863,744.76.

observations.reason: The LGU did not submit the complete supporting documents.

observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 page 40
observations.reason: Management continued the practice of processing of honoraria despite the absence of the documents needed to support the claim.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) strictly adhere to the provisions of R.A 9003 and implement the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan they had prepared and allot a budget for the program; (b) as a first step, put up receptacles that are well categorized and segregated and immediately take a comprehensive program to address garbage disposal that would ultimately eliminate the existing practice; and (c) take appropriate actions that would lead to the closure of the open dump and switch to sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management implement the projects funded from the 20% Local Development Fund so that the constituents can benefit from positive outcome that may be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 page 48 Restated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
‘We recommended that Management (a) create an Inventory Committee to
facilitate the inventory count of all the LGU’s assets; and (b) reconcile the report
with the accounting records to identify any discrepancies that may require
adjustment.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers together with its
complete supporting documents in order that a proper review of transactions can be
made.

‘We recommended that Management:

() stop the granting of additional cash advances to Accountable Officers and
employees unless the previous ones are fully settled;

(b) institute the appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those erring
accountable officers/employees for continued non-compliance with the

29
existing laws, rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and
liquidation of cash advances, if warranted; and

(c) ensure that the granting utilization and liquidation of cash advances is in
accordance with the provision of COA Circular No. 97-002.

‘We recommended that Management direct the Accounting Office to trace the
difference and reconcile the reciprocal accounts, and thereafter effect adjustments
to reflect the correct account balances and present reliable data on its financial
operation as of a given period of time.

‘We recommended that the Management:

(a) require the LGU personnel to submit a copy of print-out DTR from
biometric device for CY 2016 as proof of their attendance;

(b) issue a certification that the activities/services cannot be provided by
regular or permanent personnel of the agency before employing job
orders/contract of service personnel;

(c) require each payces to prepare individual accomplishment report duly
verified and signed by the immediate supervisor;

32
(d) refrain from submitting a duplicate/scan copy of DTR and
Accomplishment Report;

(e) indicate the number and date of their CTCs as a required identification
on the payroll;

(f) indicate the statement of duties and responsibilities of each personnel; and

(g) evaluate the necessity of getting the services of job orders and comsider
terminating those services whose functions are redundant to the functions of
the different officers and/or offices in order that unnecessary expenditures be
minimize, if not totally avoided.

We recommended that Management: (a) observe prudence in the
disbursements of the LGU’s budget and always keep track of the charges in the
budget to prevent overspending; and (b) limit travel and the number of employees
ordered to travel to those which are necessary for proper administration.

We recommended that Management:

(a) direct the concerned officials to fast track the distribution of relief goods
intended for the victims to ensure that assistance is extended to rightful
beneficiaries;

(b) observe proper procurement planning, implementation and menitoring
of relief goods that would aid in making decision as to the necessity of
procuring additional relief goods during disaster relief operations in order to
avoid huge balances of stocks after each relief operation; and

(¢) adhere strictly to the provisions of NDRRMC-DBM & DILG JMC No.
2013-1.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the balances of all inter-agency payables in order to
establish the correctness and accuracy of the accounts.

We recommended that Management require the submission of liquidation
reports for fund transfers and/or refund the unutilized balances for completed
projects in order to show the reliable balance of the liability accounts.

We recommended that Management study the packages offered by mobile
networks and evaluate which would provide the best price favorable to the
government. We likewise recommend that Management refrain from .paying
excessive expenditures as required under COA Circular No. 2012-003 and see to it
that claims are supported with adequate documentation.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
(a) strictly adhere to the requirements of RA 3720 and COA Circular No. 2012-001
before processing claims on drugs and medicines; and (b) require the Bids and
Awards Committee to submit the required documents to forestall the issuance of
Notice of Suspension on the procured items totaling 470,504.28.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers complete with description of every
transaction of the Municipality to ascertain correctness and validity of the accounts.

To promptly implement all the programs/projects/activities as embodied in
the approved Annual Development Plan in order to optimize the delivery of basic
services to the people, we recommended that Management:

(a.) require the Municipal Development Council to judiciously plan the

programs/projects/activities to be funded out of the 20% EDF in accordance
with the mandate of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011; and

(b.) prosecute the projects through straight contract with terms and
conditions indicating definite date of completion.

We  recommended  that Management: (a) require the Property Custodian Designate/Head of the Committee on Inventory of PPE to prepare and submit a properly and completely accomplished/ filled-up RPCPPE based on a physical count conducted; (b) assign value to PPEs acquired through donation; (c) update the AREs; and (d) maintain property cards for each type of PPE.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 page 26 Restated in Part I of this report.
observations.reason: Due to lack of supperting documents, Management failed to assign corresponding amount to some PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) refrain from granting additional cash advances to employees with existing cash advances; (b) send demand letters to employees with long outstanding cash advances and demand settlement thereof, and (c) ensure that the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances is in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 page 29 Restated in Part II of this report.
observations.reason: Various LGU activities and program requires additional cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the officers of the two grantee associations to submit the Approved Summary of Budgetary Requirements detailing the items of expenditure/disbursements to be incurred in the program/project and liquidation reports on the utilization of the financial assistance granted.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 page 32
observations.reason: No supporting documents were submitted by the Lagonoy North District Teachers and Employees Association.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (1) designate a social worker who will do the evaluating process in determining the eligibility of the prospective beneficiares; (2) follow strictly the guidelines in the implementation of AICS program as provided under DSWD AO No. 5; (3) Ensure that claims for financial assistance are properly supported with valid and complete documents; and (4) require the submission of the noted lacking documents and justification on the excessive grants to forestall the issuance of Notices of Suspension.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 page 33
observations.reason: LGU failed to designate a social worker who will do the evaluation process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the concerned labarers to refund the excess amount of salaries paid to them to forestall the issuance of Notices of Disallowance wherein the personnel who prepared and approved the payrolls shall also be named as person liable.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 page 37

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop the practice of paying maintenance and other operating expenses in cash and strictly comply with the general principle of COA Circular No. 97-002 that all payments must be made by check under the imprest system.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 page 41

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management to be diligent in observing the guidelines for the disbursement of calamity funds and strictly adhere to RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 page 42

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers together with its complete supporting documents in order that a proper review of transactions can be achieved.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 page 46
observations.reason: Restated in Part I of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect adjustments in the affected asset account.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 page 47
